title: comforting Malty Velvety Argentinian Beef Stew
description: A hearty, comforting stew made with traditional Argentinian spices and served in a warm earthenware bowl.
instructions: Heat the beef fat in a large Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, stirring occasionally.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chili flakes, cumin, and paprika and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the beef shin and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the beef stock, red wine, and browned beef to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer to the preheated oven. Braise the stew for 2 hours and 30 minutes, or until the beef is tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the flour to thicken the stew.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or thyme, if desired.
Strain the stew through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
Skim off any excess fat that rises to the surface, if desired.
